assertions.text: Vehicle was stationary (not moving) at the time of the collision.
The vehicle was stationary and waiting before the single impact occurred.
There was a single impact (one distinct collision) to the car.
A heavy impact struck the car (the car was struck by another object rather than initiating the impact).

verdicts.evidence: The claim states the vehicle was stationary at impact, but GPS shows non-zero speed samples around the impact: [47.0 km/h at t=11.0](T3) and [38.0 km/h at t=12.0](T2) and compute_stats reports max GPS speed [47.0 km/h and last_value 2.0 km/h over window](T4). The gps samples indicate the vehicle was moving at impact time, contradicting stationary.

The assertion that the vehicle was stationary and waiting before the impact is contradicted by GPS speed samples showing the vehicle moving prior to impact, e.g. [45.0 km/h at t=10.0] and [47.0 km/h at t=11.0] (T3) and detect_events reports a speed drop from [45.0 km/h to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0 and t=13.0] (T2).

Detect_events found a single merged impact spike on accel_x with peak at [t=11.01 s magnitude 5.86 g] and one on accel_y at [t=11.01 s magnitude 2.67 g], indicating one distinct collision event at [t≈11.01 s] (T2).

Telemetry shows a large impulsive acceleration on device axes at [t=11.01 s with accel_x peak -5.862 g and accel_y -2.665 g] (T4), but device-to-vehicle axis alignment and sign conventions are undocumented so whether the car was struck by another object (rather than initiating contact) cannot be determined from sensors alone (T4, T2).

The impact produced large peak accelerations with accel_x peak signed deviation [−5.862 g at t=11.01 s] and accel_z peak abs deviation [3.816 g at t=11.03 s], and compute_stats reports an integrated device-frame delta‑V estimate of [−54.4 km/h on accel_x], indicating a heavy/severe impact pulse on the vehicle (T4, T2).

result.rationale: GPS shows the vehicle was moving at impact, contradicting the claimant's stationary assertions (a1,a2). Sensors do support a single, high‑magnitude impact (a3,a5), while impact direction (who struck whom) is unverifiable due to unknown axis alignment (a4).

citations.claim: 47.0 km/h and last_value 2.0 km/h over window

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
